plan: Cut all leveraged ETFs like SOXL and SPAL to zero.
Sell SMH as it is in a downtrend to reduce semiconductor risk.
Increase QQQI to 25% for income generation and stability.
Reduce SPY to 20% to lower broad index concentration.
Buy Microsoft (MSFT) at 10% as a high-quality tech addition.
This plan prioritizes capital preservation with a large cash buffer.
conservative.read: This plan aims for low monthly growth around 1.5% with reduced volatility. It cuts leveraged ETFs and concentrated positions, favoring income and quality names to preserve capital.

plan: Trim SOXL to 2% and sell SPAL to reduce leveraged ETF exposure.
Sell SMH as it is in a downtrend to cut semiconductor risk.
Reduce QQQI to 15% and SPY to 25% to lower concentration.
Increase CRWD to 15% to lean into a strong performer.
Buy Microsoft (MSFT) at 10% as a quality tech addition.
This plan balances growth with risk reduction, maintaining a moderate cash buffer.
balanced.read: This plan targets around 2.5% monthly growth by reducing leverage and concentration. It trims underperformers and over-concentrated names while adding to strong performers and quality tech.

plan: Increase SOXL to 10% and add SPAL at 5% to embrace leveraged ETFs.
Sell SMH as it is in a downtrend to free up capital.
Trim QQQI to 5% and SPY to 20% to reduce broad index exposure.
Increase CRWD to 15% to further concentrate in a strong performer.
Buy GUSH at 5% as it offers a high risk/reward setup.
This plan maximizes growth potential by leaning into leverage and concentration.
aggressive.read: This plan targets aggressive growth of around 4% per month by using and adding leveraged ETFs and concentrating in high-conviction setups. It carries significantly higher risk, including amplified drawdowns and potential for rapid losses due to leverage and ETF rebalancing.

plan: Sell all leveraged ETFs (SOXL, SPAL) and high-beta tech (CRWD).
Sell SMH as it is in a downtrend.
Increase QQQI to 40% for maximum income generation.
Add SPY to 30% to capture broad market income distributions.
This plan prioritizes stable monthly income and capital preservation.
It aims for reliable distributions with significantly lower volatility.
income.read: This plan focuses on generating steady monthly income around 1% with very low volatility. It shifts the portfolio entirely to income-oriented ETFs and broad indices, cutting all leveraged and high-beta single names.
